title: ballota saxatilis subsp. brachyodonta
intro: Ballota saxatilis subsp. brachyodonta, commonly known as short-stalked ballota, is a subspecies of the Ballota saxatilis plant, a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae). This perennial herb has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as indigestion and flatulence, due to its carminative and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Ballota saxatilis subsp. brachyodonta include its ability to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or teas made from the leaves, which can be consumed as a treatment for various health issues, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
